heading: Per diem and subsistence expenses from Senate contingent fund
label: 6513 Per diem and subsistence expenses from Senate contingent fund
text: No part of the appropriations made under the heading “Contingent Expenses of the Senate” on and after may be expended for per diem and subsistence expenses (as defined in ) at rates in excess of the rates prescribed by the Committee on Rules and Administration; except that (1) higher rates may be established by the Committee on Rules and Administration for travel beyond the limits of the continental United States, and (2) in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate, reimbursement for such expenses may be made on an actual expense basis of not to exceed the daily rate prescribed by the Committee on Rules and Administration in the case of travel within the continental limits of the United States. This section shall not apply with respect to per diem or actual travel expenses incurred by Senators and employees in the office of a Senator which are reimbursed under .June 27, 1956section 5701 of title 5section 6314 of this title
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section. Some section numbers of this title referenced in amendment notes below reflect the classification of such sections prior to their editorial reclassification.section 68b of this title

Amendments1980— substituted “prescribed by the Committee on Rules and Administration” for “in effect under , for employees of agencies” in two places.Pub. L. 96–304section 5702 of title 51978— substituted provisions relating to applicability of rates under , for employees of agencies, for provisions setting forth specific rates of $35 and $50 per day, respectively, for travel expenses.Pub. L. 95–355section 5702 of title 51977— inserted provisions relating to applicability to per diem or actual travel expenses incurred by a Senator or his employee reimbursed under .Pub. L. 95–94section 58 of this title1975— substituted “$35” and “$50” for “$25” and “$40”, respectively.Pub. L. 94–221969— increased maximum per diem rate from $16 to $25 and actual expense rate from $30 to $40.Pub. L. 91–1141961— increased maximum per diem rate from $12 to $16 and actual expense rate from $25 to $30.Pub. L. 87–139

Effective Date of 1977 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1977 Amendment note under .Pub. L. 95–94section 112(f) of Pub. L. 95–94section 6314 of this titleAug. 5, 1977
